Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify Debra Vernon

Off Calendar Dec-18-2025 Continued To Jan-12-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-04-2025 Continued To Feb-10-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-05-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Vernon's demurrer to the cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty is sustained withoutleave to amend. (CCP 430.10(e).)

The court grants Vernon's unopposed request for judicial notice.

(see word minutes for complete details)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Visiting Judge: Cindee Mayfield; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CM)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-05-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JOYNUP INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant Joynup Inc.'s motion to strike is denied. The parties' alleged settlement discussions are irrelevant to this pleading motion.

Per CCP 452, the court liberally construes the First Amended Complaint. A motion to strike may "[s]trike out any irrelevant, false, or improper matter." (CCP 436(a).) In this case, the fiduciary duty allegations are not irrelevant since the First Amended Complaint attempted to plead a claim for breach of fiduciary duty against defendant Vernon. Incorporating general charging allegations by reference for the entire complaint is a standard practice and does not prejudice Joynup. (See PH II, Inc. v. Superior Court (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1680 (1683 ["We emphasize that such use of the motion to strike should be cautious and sparing. We have no intention of creating a procedural 'line item veto' for the civil defendant."].) Plaintiff sufficiently alleges a basis for the prayer for attorney's fees based on section 7.10 of the Master Services Agreement. (First Amended Complaint, par. 27.)

Order signed.

Visiting Judge: Cindee Mayfield; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CM)
